heading: Multi-State plans
label: 18054 Multi-State plans

children.heading: Oversight by the Office of Personnel Management
children.label: a Oversight by the Office of Personnel Management

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Director of the Office of Personnel Management (referred to in this section as the “Director”) shall enter into contracts with health insurance issuers (which may include a group of health insurance issuers affiliated either by common ownership and control or by the common use of a nationally licensed service mark), without regard to or other statutes requiring competitive bidding, to offer at least 2 multi-State qualified health plans through each Exchange in each State. Such plans shall provide individual, or in the case of small employers, group coverage.section 6101 of title 41

children.heading: Terms
children.label: 2 Terms
children.text: Each contract entered into under paragraph (1) shall be for a uniform term of at least 1 year, but may be made automatically renewable from term to term in the absence of notice of termination by either party. In entering into such contracts, the Director shall ensure that health benefits coverage is provided in accordance with the types of coverage provided for under section 2701(a)(1)(A)(i) of the Public Health Service Act [].42 U.S.C. 300gg(a)(1)(A)(i)

children.heading: Non-profit entities
children.label: 3 Non-profit entities
children.text: In entering into contracts under paragraph (1), the Director shall ensure that at least one contract is entered into with a non-profit entity.

children.label: A
children.text: a medical loss ratio;

children.label: B
children.text: a profit margin;

children.label: C
children.text: the premiums to be charged; and

children.label: D
children.text: such other terms and conditions of coverage as are in the interests of enrollees in such plans.

children.heading: Authority to protect consumers
children.label: 5 Authority to protect consumers
children.text: The Director may prohibit the offering of any multi-State health plan that does not meet the terms and conditions defined by the Director with respect to the elements described in subparagraphs (A) through (D) of paragraph (4).

children.heading: Assured availability of varied coverage
children.label: 6 Assured availability of varied coverage
children.text: In entering into contracts under this subsection, the Director shall ensure that with respect to multi-State qualified health plans offered in an Exchange, there is at least one such plan that does not provide coverage of services described in .section 18023(b)(1)(B)(i) of this title

children.heading: Withdrawal
children.label: 7 Withdrawal
children.text: Approval of a contract under this subsection may be withdrawn by the Director only after notice and opportunity for hearing to the issuer concerned without regard to subchapter II of chapter 5 and chapter 7 of title 5.
agrees to offer a multi-State qualified health plan that meets the requirements of subsection (c) in each Exchange in each State;

is licensed in each State and is subject to all requirements of State law not inconsistent with this section, including the standards and requirements that a State imposes that do not prevent the application of a requirement of part A of title XXVII of the Public Health Service Act [ et seq.] or a requirement of this title;42 U.S.C. 300gg3See References in Text note below.3

otherwise complies with the minimum standards prescribed for carriers offering health benefits plans under to the extent that such standards do not conflict with a provision of this title; andsection 8902(e) of title 53
meets such other requirements as determined appropriate by the Director, in consultation with the Secretary.

children.heading: Requirements for multi-State qualified health plan
children.label: c Requirements for multi-State qualified health plan
A
children.text: the plan offers a benefits package that is uniform in each State and consists of the essential benefits described in ;section 18022 of this title

children.label: B
children.text: the plan meets all requirements of this title with respect to a qualified health plan, including requirements relating to the offering of the bronze, silver, and gold levels of coverage and catastrophic coverage in each State Exchange;3

children.label: C
children.text: except as provided in paragraph (5), the issuer provides for determinations of premiums for coverage under the plan on the basis of the rating requirements of part A of title XXVII of the Public Health Service Act; and

children.label: D
children.text: the issuer offers the plan in all geographic regions, and in all States that have adopted adjusted community rating before .March 23, 2010

children.heading: States may offer additional benefits
children.label: 2 States may offer additional benefits
children.text: Nothing in paragraph (1)(A) shall preclude a State from requiring that benefits in addition to the essential health benefits required under such paragraph be provided to enrollees of a multi-State qualified health plan offered in such State.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: An individual enrolled in a multi-State qualified health plan under this section shall be eligible for credits under and cost sharing assistance under in the same manner as an individual who is enrolled in a qualified health plan.section 36B of title 26section 18071 of this title

children.heading: No additional Federal cost
children.label: B No additional Federal cost
children.text: A requirement by a State under paragraph (2) that benefits in addition to the essential health benefits required under paragraph (1)(A) be provided to enrollees of a multi-State qualified health plan shall not affect the amount of a premium tax credit provided under with respect to such plan.section 36B of title 26

children.label: A
children.text: to an individual enrolled in a multi-State qualified health plan offered in such State; or

children.label: B
children.text: on behalf of an individual described in subparagraph (A) directly to the multi-State qualified health plan in which such individual is enrolled;

children.heading: Application of certain State rating requirements
children.label: 5 Application of certain State rating requirements
children.text: With respect to a multi-State qualified health plan that is offered in a State with age rating requirements that are lower than 3:1, the State may require that Exchanges operating in such State only permit the offering of such multi-State qualified health plans if such plans comply with the State’s more protective age rating requirements.

children.heading: Plans deemed to be certified
children.label: d Plans deemed to be certified
children.text: A multi-State qualified health plan that is offered under a contract under subsection (a) shall be deemed to be certified by an Exchange for purposes of .section 18031(d)(4)(A) of this title
with respect to the first year for which the issuer offers such plan, such issuer offers the plan in at least 60 percent of the States;
with respect to the second such year, such issuer offers the plan in at least 70 percent of the States;
with respect to the third such year, such issuer offers the plan in at least 85 percent of the States; and
with respect to each subsequent year, such issuer offers the plan in all States.

children.heading: Applicability
children.label: f Applicability
children.text: The requirements under chapter 89 of title 5 applicable to health benefits plans under such chapter shall apply to multi-State qualified health plans provided for under this section to the extent that such requirements do not conflict with a provision of this title.3

children.heading: Maintenance of effort
children.label: 1 Maintenance of effort
children.text: Nothing in this section shall be construed to permit the Director to allocate fewer financial or personnel resources to the functions of the Office of Personnel Management related to the administration of the Federal Employees Health Benefit Program under chapter 89 of title 5.

children.heading: Separate risk pool
children.label: 2 Separate risk pool
children.text: Enrollees in multi-State qualified health plans under this section shall be treated as a separate risk pool apart from enrollees in the Federal Employees Health Benefit Program under chapter 89 of title 5.

children.heading: Authority to establish separate entities
children.label: 3 Authority to establish separate entities
children.text: The Director may establish such separate units or offices within the Office of Personnel Management as the Director determines to be appropriate to ensure that the administration of multi-State qualified health plans under this section does not interfere with the effective administration of the Federal Employees Health Benefit Program under chapter 89 of title 5.

children.heading: Effective oversight
children.label: 4 Effective oversight
children.text: The Director may appoint such additional personnel as may be necessary to enable the Director to carry out activities under this section.

children.heading: Assurance of separate program
children.label: 5 Assurance of separate program
children.text: In carrying out this section, the Director shall ensure that the program under this section is separate from the Federal Employees Health Benefit Program under chapter 89 of title 5. Premiums paid for coverage under a multi-State qualified health plan under this section shall not be considered to be Federal funds for any purposes.

children.heading: FEHBP plans not required to participate
children.label: 6 FEHBP plans not required to participate
children.text: Nothing in this section shall require that a carrier offering coverage under the Federal Employees Health Benefit Program under chapter 89 of title 5 also offer a multi-State qualified health plan under this section.

children.heading: Advisory board
children.label: h Advisory board
children.text: The Director shall establish an advisory board to provide recommendations on the activities described in this section. A significant percentage of the members of such board shall be comprised of enrollees in a multi-State qualified health plan, or representatives of such enrollees.

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: i Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There is authorized to be appropriated, such sums as may be necessary to carry out this section.
